segments.text: Then Elihu continued,
Listen to my words, you men who say you are wise; pay attention to what I'm saying you who think you know.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: The ear distinguishes words just as the palate distinguishes foods.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Let us discern for ourselves what is right: let us decide among ourselves what is good.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Job said, ‘I am innocent, and God has denied me justice.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Even though I'm right, I'm treated like a liar; I am dying from my wounds, even though I've done nothing wrong.’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Has there ever been a man like Job with such a thirst for ridiculing others?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: He keeps company with wicked people; he associates with those who do evil.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: He's even said, ‘What benefit is there in being God's friend?’
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: So listen to me, you men of understanding! It's impossible for God to do evil and the Almighty to do wrong.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: He pays people back for what they've done, and treats them as they deserve.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: It's absolutely sure that God doesn't act wickedly; the Almighty would never pervert justice.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Who put him in charge of the earth? Who gave him the responsibility for all the world?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: If he were to withdraw his spirit, if he were to take back his breath,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: all living things would immediately die and human beings would return to dust.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: If you have understanding then hear this; pay attention to what I'm saying.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Do you really think someone who hated justice could govern? Are you going to condemn Almighty God who always does what is right?
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: He's the one who tells kings, ‘You're useless!’ or says to nobles, ‘You're wicked!’
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: He doesn't think more of the rich than the poor, for they are all people he himself made.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: They die in a moment; at midnight they shudder and pass away; the mighty are gone without effort.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: For he watches what they're doing and sees everywhere they go.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: There is no darkness so deep where people who do evil can hide themselves from him.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: God doesn't need to examine anyone in any greater detail that they should come before him for judgment.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: He brings down the mighty without needing an investigation; he sets up others in their place.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Knowing what they've done he overthrows them in a night and destroys them.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: He strikes them down for their wickedness in public where they can be seen
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: because they turned away from following him, disregarding all his ways.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: They made the poor call out to him, and he heard the cries of the oppressed.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Yet if God wants to remain silent, who can condemn him? If he chooses to hide his face, who can see him? Whether it concerns a nation or an individual,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: a person who rejects God should not rule so they don't mislead people.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: If you were to say to God, ‘I have sinned, but I won't do bad things any more.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Show me what I cannot see. If I have done wrong, I won't do it again,’
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: then should God reward you for following your own opinions since you have rejected his? You're the one who has to choose, not me! Tell us what you think.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: For people who understand—those who are wise who have heard what I said—will tell me,
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: ‘Job doesn't know what he's talking about. What he says doesn't make any sense.’
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: If only Job were thoroughly condemned because he speaks like evil people do.
segments.speaker: Elihu

segments.text: Now he has added rebellion to his sins; he claps his hands at us,  making long speeches full of accusations against God.
segments.speaker: Elihu
